item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh this is nice, although she feels more akin to Green Lantern than Captain Marvel

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alpha-9 is in my head canon, so I'm personally psyched to see an SCP besides Iris on board. That said, great article. I like the link to actual physics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is cute. Both 4818 and the researcher feel like actual people. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i love her

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed this article! I've always felt it was unfair for a humanoid anomaly to be down voted on the pretense of the X-Man Syndrome (I certainly understand the reasoning behind it, but still) I think any human being, especially one so young, would immediately identify themselves with the pop culture figures that are so prevalent in our day and age. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll leave it up to someone who's already contributed to Resurrection to determine if this qualifies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't this be tagged under Ressurection? It heavily mentions Alpha-9 a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This sounds like one of the few SCPs that I'd actually enjoy being around. And it's nice to finally see an anomalous human being treated like, you know, a human. Have my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: really enjoyed this, with a lot of entries having similar build up to this one usually ending up with something tragic happening, this was quite wholesome. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My main man Mr. Fish, god I haven’t seen anything about him in forever. Anyways a very heart warming story. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's rather nice to see Alpha-9 here. Haven't heard that name in a long time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Behold the power of headcanon.

That sounds needlessly convoluted, but you do you. I'm glad you liked it, even if it goes against your personal headcanon of how the Foundation acts.

My personal headcanon is that it's in the Foundations best interest to keep the SCPs sane and co-operative, which means concessions for their psychological well being must be made. This means that non-hostile SCPs are treated more like residents at a longterm care facility than prisoners. Yes, there's always the possibility that one will be the next SCP-1048, but you can't lock ever anomaly in solitary confinement because of one evil teddy bear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, this one's hard for me to vote on.

On the one hand, I think the girl's reactions are very realistic, as is her dialog.

On the other hand, I just don't think it makes sense for the scientists to be behaving this way. From the Foundation's perspective, what are they getting out of this? They've had so many anomalies like Zachary Callahan or the love slug that deceive people, or the radio cat and mirror that were both planted by the Chaos Insurgency. So why let her wander around? Why introduce her to other entities and let them use powers with each other? It all makes sense as a story about her, but not as a story about the Foundation.

For those reasons, and for consistency, I'm going to downvote it, but I think it's great writing, and I like all of your stuff. To make for it, I'm going to go through and upvote all your stuff I liked that I forgot to upvote before.

Edit: After careful consideration, I've changed it to an up vote. I'm still keeping all the other up votes, though. I especially like the SCP-999 and dado stories.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I relayed some of this to you in IRC, but for the sake of "I didn't really see it mentioned here yet" there's a lot of things that make me tentative about my upvote. I did like it, and I have no qualms with X-Man syndrome, but I think this ended up being just too nice. Like, I think you did the "permission to do good guy stuffz" and all to actually make it feel like maybe less of a Mary Sue, like not so much of a good character juxtaposed against the "horrors of the Foundation" or some shit, but it kinda has the opposite effect.

Honestly, my biggest complaint is just the missed potential. I know the "Foundation is evil" is hella tropey and overused, but it doesn't have to be evil per se. I don't think anybody really wants to be entrapped, especially as a youth. Your character accepts this way too easily. If I went to prison even though I didn't do anything wrong, I wouldn't be like "oh hey, I met somebody who was relatively nice to me, so I'm cool with this whole prison thing now". That's not how people work, especially teenagers. Everybody wants to be free yo.

I dunno. I still liked it but that was such a teetering +1/novote. I think the only reason I resolved at a +1 is because of course, I can never fault somebody for not ending something the way I personally would have because that would be incredibly self-centered of me. I feel like I just bit into a Reese's cup and instead of peanut butter, there was just more chocolate. Yeah, it was chocolate, which is great, but I wanted some peanut butter too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this was a great approach to the whole superhero skip idea. The SCP felt real and relatable while not being perfect. But like other people above me have said the blocked interview feels very unneeded and disrupts the closure for me. Besides that great addition.

+1

Btw were you inspired by SCP-4051 in any way?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
gotta agree with some previous points on this. I like it a lot, and liked that it wasn't too Mary Sue-ish. +1 from me. I gotta agree with plaguebearer's point about the researcher saying good girl, it just made my skin crawl. The implications are a lot less troublesome as a motherly figure, but you may want to rethink that phrase if you want the researcher to be as warm as I think you do.

It may just be because I say "good girl" to my pets. Maybe something like "that's my girl" or "that's the spirit"?

Otherwise, it does feel a bit unsatisfactory that I can't expand the alpha-9 thing. I get your motivation for making inaccessible, and I'm not helpful/creative enough to come up with an alternative, but I think there could be a way of having this redacted experience without making us feel so dissatisfied.

Overall, this is a good entry, I appreciate the "warm" approach to a researcher.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this is cool and all, but 3009 did the "Life inside the Foundation" examination better IMO.

also, i get chills whenever someone says good girl, even if it's not used in a nasty way.

hesitant upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You wrote the character too well for that :D I wanted to see her being accepted to alpha 9 and being happy :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was just going for an old school "Data Expunged" moment, but one that was still logically justifiable since it makes sense that Alpha 9's activities would be classified information.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice heartwarming article. Another example that you can make a pitfall/trope/cliche article if executed well. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I started reading this I thought this was going to be too, well, Mary-Sue-ish. But I think you did a good job of balancing the sadness of being ripped away from your family and declared dead to the outside world with the excitement a teenager would feel upon getting a chance to be an actual hero. Provided she follows all the rules, of course.

Cold but not cruel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it very mutch!
The Doctor/Resercher was cool, not so cold like some others

item.title: Photon Lady!
item.content:encoded: Personally I don't have any problems with superhero-themed SCPs! Me likey +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I did feel the font is a bit hard to read (sentences do not look like they have spaces between them and the "j-dot" is not really visible).

Two mistakes I noticed:

I didn't even know it wasn't something other people couldn't feel.

a better idea that putting me in the same room with an invincible, ancient cyborg.

Overall I really enjoyed this though! She is a pretty likeable character and I am glad you made the Foundation actually care for her. So it is a +1 from me.

But why did you deny access to the last interview? D: Why put it there if the reader can't enjoy what I expected to be the culmination of all interviews?

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
So we all know about X-man syndrome, about how superhero SCPs tend not to be received very well. This is my attempt to make a Super Skip that works.

This is also my first time experimenting with the new site theme and interview divs instead of the usual dotted box. If you have any suggestions to make them better, like a better font (I'm not 100 percent satisfied with the font it has now) please feel free.

Edit: I changed the font to Helvetica for now. I think it looks better.

Thanks to my beta readers: CaptainKirby, Elogee, IamtheOoga, Ihp, Metaphysician, Organic_Panic, Quantanuat, Rattles, and TherealSEC.
